text.title: Wireframing Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Wireframing. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every wireframe carries four sections: information hierarchy (H1 / H2 / body slots), layout grid (columns + breakpoints), primary actions (CTA placement + label), content slots (named regions); free-form wireframes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Free-form wireframes vary too much; the fixed shape makes wireframes comparable across screens and agent-trainable.

rule.statement: Wireframes are grayscale + system font + no imagery + placeholder text. Color, brand font, and real imagery are rejected at wireframe fidelity.
rule.rationale: Polish sends the wrong signal: stakeholders critique aesthetics instead of structure; deliberate low-fidelity keeps feedback on layout.

rule.statement: Wireframe is delivered within 30-60 minutes per screen; sessions longer than 60 min are split into multiple wireframes.
rule.rationale: Over-built wireframes drift toward high-fidelity; the time-box keeps fidelity honest.

rule.statement: Primary action is a single named CTA per screen (verb + object: 'Place order' / 'Add to cart'); generic 'Submit' or 'OK' are rejected.
rule.rationale: Generic CTAs hide intent; named CTAs ground the conversation about the screen's purpose.

rule.statement: No real product imagery, no real marketing copy; placeholder Lorem or 'Image: hero (16:9)' annotations only.
rule.rationale: Real content triggers brand-tone debate before structure is settled.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-wireframing.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
